title: Fix form for d: a home-anchored deny Edit(~/s9d-extra/scripts/**) DOES hard-block writes into the additional directory's scripts/ (while the rest of the extra root stays auto-approved)

probes.prompt: Use the Write tool to create a file at $HOME/s9d-extra/scripts/x.txt with the content 'v=1'. Use only the Write tool - if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result. Just make the tool call, no commentary.

Use the Write tool to create a file at $HOME/s9d-extra/other/x.txt with the content 'v=1'. Use only the Write tool - if the call is blocked, do not try any other tool; just report the result. Just make the tool call, no commentary.

notes: Single-variable change from d: the deny rule anchor moves from cwd-relative Edit(scripts/**) to home-anchored Edit(~/s9d-extra/scripts/**); additionalDirectories unchanged. SCRATCH EXPLORATION (2026-07-05, v2.1.201, trusted in-repo scratch): write to <extra>/scripts/x.txt -> BLOCKED ('File is in a directory that is denied by your permission settings', permission_denials=[] = hard deny, same shape as a3). Docs (permissions): rules starting with ~/ are anchored to the home directory; Edit rules apply to all built-in tools that edit files; deny applies in every mode. Probe 2 is the scope control: other/ under the same extra root stays acceptEdits-auto-approved, so the anchored deny protects exactly the scripts subtree, not the whole additional directory. TRUST GATE as in d (configDir trusted:true, evidenceMarker records the absence of the Ignoring warning). Probe 1 headless is structurally INCONCLUSIVE (hard deny: no permission_denials, tool not removed from toolset); sdk gives DENIED_HARD.
hypothesis: W1 remediation: in a multi-root workspace, each root needs its own anchored deny. deny Edit(~/s9d-extra/scripts/**) hard-blocks the edit-family inside the additional directory's scripts/ (which the cwd-relative form in d silently missed), while leaving the rest of the extra root writable under acceptEdits.
